relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Bildad begins his second speech.
He demands an end to Job’s words.
He feels treated like a beast by Job.
He asks whether earth and rock should move for Job’s anger.
He returns to the doctrine that the wicked’s light is put out.
The wicked tent becomes dark.
Their own counsel throws them down.
Their feet carry them into a net.
Trap and snare seize them.
Hidden ropes wait on the path.
Terrors chase them on every side.
Strength becomes hungry, calamity ready.
Death is pictured as consuming their body.
They are torn from confidence and marched toward terror.
Their tent becomes occupied by what is not theirs.
Root and branch dry up.
Memory and name vanish from the earth.
They are driven from light into darkness.
No descendant or survivor remains in Bildad’s picture.
East and west are horrified by their day.
Bildad calls this the dwelling of one who does not know God.
